conversations.content: Today we studied the Star Middle School Student Attendance Management Regulations. If a student is late for no reason once...
Late once? Let's see what the punishment is.
then the homeroom teacher gives a verbal warning and records a deduction of 1 point.
Verbal warning plus 1 point deduction — a reminder. What if they keep being late?
If a student is late for no reason three times, then issue a public notice of criticism and request a parent-teacher meeting.
Three times gets a public notice plus parent meeting — stepping up! What about truancy?
If a student is truant for one class period, then issue a demerit and revoke their eligibility for monthly awards.
One class period of truancy leads to a demerit? That's much more serious than being late! What about three consecutive days of truancy?
If a student is truant for three consecutive days, then place them on probation and report to the education bureau for record.
Probation plus reporting to the education bureau — that's getting serious!

On-the-spot stop plus 10-point deduction — a warning penalty. What about bringing cheat sheets?

Zero plus serious warning — that's serious! What about using a phone to cheat?
If a student uses electronic devices such as mobile phones to cheat, then all subject scores are nullified, a major demerit is issued, and all award eligibility is revoked.
Phone cheating — nullify all scores plus major demerit, awards all gone — harsh enough! What about organizing group cheating or stealing exam papers?
If a student organizes group cheating or steals exam papers, then they are expelled and handed over to the public security authorities.
Expulsion plus handover to police — that's criminal!

Confiscate until dismissal plus record — a reminder. What about using it in class to play games or watch videos?
If a student uses their phone during class to play games or watch videos, then confiscate the phone for one week and have a parent pick it up, and deduct 10 conduct points.
Confiscate for a week plus parent pickup, and deduct 10 conduct points — much heavier! What about photographing others' privacy or distributing inappropriate content?

Privacy photos or inappropriate distribution — demerit plus deletion, legal issues involved! What about repeated offenders who refuse to correct?

then the homeroom teacher gives criticism education and orders a written apology, and records it in the behavior file.
Criticism education plus written apology, and a record in the file — a permanent mark. What about pushing, kicking, or minor physical bullying?

Minor physical bullying gets a serious warning plus training — much heavier than verbal education! What about gathering groups to beat, extort, or threaten?

Ganging up, extortion, threats — major demerit, suspension, plus counseling — that's very serious! What about using weapons to injure, filming and spreading bullying videos, or causing the victim to self-harm or commit suicide?

memory: Student Dormitory Public Security and Emergency Incident Response Plan: If a group brawl is found in the dormitory area, then the dorm supervisor immediately calls the campus security office, then forcefully intervenes to control the situation on site; if it is verified that controlled knives or dangerous weapons are hidden in a dormitory, then the security officer confiscates them on the spot, then conducts a surprise search of the entire dormitory; if suspicious outsiders are detected sneaking into the dormitory building to commit crimes, then activate the building's access control anti-tailgate lock, then organize floor monitors to conduct floor-by-floor searches to clear the building; if any of the above situations occurs, then a full curfew must be imposed on the dormitory building, all visitor access must be suspended, and a public security rectification must be carried out in coordination with the local police station.

conversations.content: Today we studied the Campus Public Facilities and Cultural Resources Protection Regulations. If a student is found intentionally destroying expensive multimedia equipment in a teaching building...
Destroying multimedia equipment? That's intentional property damage! How do you handle it on the spot?
then the instructor stops them on the spot and seizes their campus card, then calculates the equipment damage compensation.
Stop them, seize the card, calculate compensation — make them pay! Any other destructive acts?

If it is verified that someone has drawn graffiti or torn pages from rare library books, then the librarian revokes their borrowing privileges, then demands compensation of ten times the book's price.
Damaging rare books? Revoke borrowing plus tenfold compensation — hurts their wallet! What's the third scenario?

If malicious graffiti or damage to campus landscape sculptures or cultural relics is detected, then the security office retrieves surveillance footage to track the suspect, then orders them to clean and restore the item to its original condition.
Graffiti on cultural relics — surveillance tracking plus ordered cleaning — make them clean it themselves! When any of these happens, what must ultimately be done?

If any of the above situations occurs, then a disciplinary sanction of demerit or above must be given, all eligibility for this academic year's scholarships and grants must be revoked, and a campus-wide public notice must be issued.
Understood!

Any case → demerit or above + revoke scholarship eligibility + campus-wide public notice.

conversations.content: Today we studied the Student Large-Scale Activities and Gatherings Safety Management Code. If an unauthorized gathering of over a thousand people is discovered...
A thousand-person gathering without approval? That's asking for trouble! What's the immediate action?
then the campus security team immediately disperses the crowd, then interviews the event initiator and their college advisor.
Disperse the crowd and interview initiator and advisor — hold people accountable! Any other activity risks?
If a serious risk of crowd crush is detected at a large cultural or sports event, then cut the power to the stage audio and lighting, then use a loudspeaker to direct an orderly exit.
Crush risk — cut power plus loudspeaker evacuation — prevent casualties! What's the third scenario?
If inciting speech or collusion with off-campus groups is detected at a gathering, then forcibly take over the on-site public address equipment, then isolate the core instigators.
Inciting or collusion — take over PA equipment plus isolate instigators — don't let it escalate! When any of these happens, what must ultimately be done?

If any of the above situations occurs, then all related activities must be immediately halted, the campus level-1 stability maintenance response must be activated, and a special task force headed by university leadership must be formed to conduct an in-depth investigation.
Understood!

Any case → halt all related activities + activate level-1 stability response + leadership task force investigation.
